member.name: Kerry McCarthy
member.synopsis: Kerry McCarthy is the Labour MP for Bristol East , and has been an MP continually since 5 May 2005.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Textiles: Recycling
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether the forthcoming Circular Economy Strategy will include proposals to introduce Extended Producer Responsibility for the textiles sector.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government is committed to transitioning towards a circular economy where resources are kept in use for longer and waste is designed out. This systemic change, with investment in green jobs and vital infrastructure, builds a path to economic growth, ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Foster Care: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, with reference to the Answer of 10 April 2026 to Question 126120, when her Department will publish further details on the scope of its in-depth study of fostering finances.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department plans to focus on fostering allowances, fees, as well as other financial support that carers receive, and use this to inform analysis alongside existing data on recruitment and retention.To inform this work, more data is needed about the fi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Care Homes: Fees and Charges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the potential merits of reducing care home costs for elderly and disabled people.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Fee rates are set by providers of adult social care, the majority of which are in the independent sector. The Department does not have powers to set or recommend the level of fees that care homes charge. However, all businesses are required to comply with...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Affordable Housing: Bristol
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what assessment he has made of the availability of affordable housing for (a) care leavers and (b) young people unable to live with their families in Bristol.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The National Planning Policy Framework (NPPF) makes clear that local planning authorities should assess the size, type, and tenure of housing needed for different groups, and should reflect this in their planning policies and decisions.I otherwise refer t...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Affordable Housing: Young Offenders
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what discussions he has had with the Secretary of State for Justice on securing (a) affordable, (b) safe and (c) appropriate accommodation for young people leaving youth custody
writtenQuestions.answerText: There are regular discussions between cabinet colleagues, and further dialogue through the Inter-Ministerial Group on homelessness and rough sleeping. The details of these discussions are not usually disclosed.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Housing: Apprentices
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what housing cost support is available for young apprentices living independently.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Housing costs support is available in Universal Credit for those of working age who have a liability to pay rent for the home that they live in. Renters that require further support, where they face a shortfall in meeting their housing costs, can apply fo...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Nature Conservation: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the potential merits of introducing fiscal incentives to support wildlife restoration and habitat creation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committing more than £2.7 billion per year in sustainable farming and nature recovery from 2026‑27 until 2028‑29. This includes increasing support for nature-friendly farming through Environmental Land Management schemes from £800 millio...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Property Development
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what assessment he has made of the potential risk of speculative development in locations without an up-to-date Local Plan.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In the absence of an up-to-date Local Plan, there is a high likelihood that development will come forward on a piecemeal and speculative basis, with reduced public engagement and fewer guarantees that it will make the most of an area’s potential. It is fo...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Environment Protection: Investment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps are being taken to support stable governance for private investment in nature markets.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to scaling stable, high integrity markets to mobilise investment in nature recovery and sustainable farming. As detailed in the Summary of Responses to the consultation on Voluntary Carbon and Nature Market integrity, simple an...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Recycling
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, when the Circular Economy Growth Plan will be published.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government is committed to transitioning towards a circular economy where resources are kept in use for longer and waste is designed out. This systemic change, with investment in green jobs and vital infrastructure, builds a path to economic growth, ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Rivers: Pollution Control
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps her Department is taking to support the scaling up of innovative, low-cost technologies, including fungal filtration, to reduce pollutants such as E. coli, phosphorus and ni
writtenQuestions.answerText: Funding for Environmental Land Management Schemes will increase by 150% from £800 million in 23/24 to £2 billion by 28/29 and we are working actively to target this to help address water pollution, protect nature, and support thriving biodiversity across ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: UN Convention to Combat Desertification
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, whether there will be ministerial attendance at the UN Convention to Combat Desertification (COP17) in Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia in August.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We remain committed to working with our international partners to tackle desertification, land degradation and drought. Details of UK representation at the UN Convention to Combat Desertification COP17 in Ulaanbaatar in August will be confirmed in due cou...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

writtenQuestions.heading: School Support Staff Negotiating Body
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what her Department's timetable is for responding to the Setting up the School Support Staff Negotiating Body consultation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department is currently preparing the response to the public consultation on setting up the School Support Staff Negotiating Body for publication.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Prisons: Visits
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what discussions he has had with representatives from prisons on making prison visits more suitable for children and families.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Ministry of Justice Ministers have frequent discussions with prison staff on all aspects of the prison experience. His Majesty’s Prison and Probation Service (HMPPS) encourages prisons to make the experience of those visiting prisoners the ‘best it can be...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Prisons: Visits
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what discussions he has had with representatives from prisons on allowing prisoners to have virtual visits with their children through longer and high-quality video calls.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Ministry of Justice Ministers have frequent discussions with prison staff on all aspects of the prison experience. His Majesty’s Prison and Probation Service (HMPPS) encourages prisons to make the experience of those visiting prisoners the ‘best it can be...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Pregnancy Tests
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, pursuant to the answer of 9 October 2025 to Question 76643 on Primodos, whether his Department plans to consider scientific evidence from Aaron. P. Adam et al (2026) titled Recurrent Constellations
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA), together with the wider Government, have committed to review any new scientific evidence which comes to light regarding the use of hormone pregnancy tests during early pregnancy and adverse p...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Prisoners: Parents
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, if he will make an assessment of the potential merits of making support for parents in prisons a Key Performance Indicator.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Decisions on changes to prison key performance indicators, including whether to introduce new or more explicit measures focused on support for parents in prison, must balance the benefits of clearer accountability with the need to ensure performance frame...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Prisoners: Parents
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice what comparative assessment he has made of the level of support in prisons for parents on remand or serving custodial sentences with the potential impact on rehabilitation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We know that supporting and maintaining positive family relationships, where safe and appropriate, is an important factor in reducing reoffending and desistance from crime, with recently published research finding that prisoners who received visits were 3...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Advanced Therapy Medicinal Products
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether he has had discussions with colleagues in NHS England on the development of a regenerative medicine strategy.
writtenQuestions.answerText: No such discussions have taken place. The Government is committed to ensuring the United Kingdom remains a global leader in the development of advanced therapies, including regenerative medicines, to drive innovation in the National Health Service and del...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Advanced Therapy Medicinal Products
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of regenerative medicine techniques on surgical recovery.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Regenerative techniques, such as cell therapies, growth factors, and bioengineered scaffolds, can accelerate wound healing, which is particularly valuable in complex or high‑risk surgeries. NHS England maintains an overview of the advanced therapy medicin...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

items.text: Name of donor: Borneo Orangutan Survival Foundation UK Address of donor: 483 Green Lanes, London N13 4BS Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Flights (£2073.55), medical screening tests (£340.00), accommodation (£360.94) and wider expenses (£237.56), value £3,012.04 Destination of visit: Indonesia and Malaysia Dates of visit: 26 March 2026 to 10 April 2026 Purpose of visit: Conservation visit with cross-party MPs to see work of the charity in action and impact of deforestation and palm oil supply chain on Borneo, hosted by international conservation organisation (Registered 21 May 2026)

Name of donor: Tibetan Central Administration Address of donor: Tibet House, 1 Culworth Street NW8 7AF Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): International return flight London to Delhi (£900), internal return flight Delhi to Dharamshala (£170), Delhi hotel accommodation (£150) and Dharamshala hotel accommodation (£450), value £1,670 Destination of visit: India Dates of visit: 25 May 2026 to 30 May 2026 Purpose of visit: To meet with Tibetan officials, including the Dalai Lama, to discuss how we can support the cause of Tibetan independence. (Registered 11 June 2026)
